lead_profile_text: R2R deal on 2-bed unfurnished Sheffield property. Rasheed based in Manchester. Landlord asking £1,000/mo, Rasheed targeting £800/mo. Property available 1 May. Gross ~£29,400/yr (£131/night, 62% occ). At £1,000/mo rent + bills (~£400/mo): overhead ~£16,800/yr = near-zero profit. At £800/mo rent: ~£3-4k/yr net profit possible. Setup cost ~£3-5k hard to recover. Sheffield too far from Manchester. Zac advised walking away if landlord won't reduce.
emotional_profile: Entrepreneurial. Manchester-based, looking for R2R opportunities. Optimistic about STL income but constrained by landlord's rent demands. Needs 20% rent reduction from landlord — Zac acknowledged this is rare. Language: 'doesn't work', 'walk away', 'closer to Manchester', 'not much opportunity in this deal'.
lost_reason: Landlord won't reduce rent from £1,000/mo to viable ~£800/mo. At £1,000 rent profit near zero. Setup cost ~£3-5k unrecoverable. Sheffield too far from Rasheed's Manchester base.

top_objections.text: Landlord asking £1,000/mo rent — too high for R2R viability
top_objections.zac_response: At £1,000/mo rent + ~£400/mo bills, overhead ~£16,800/yr vs gross ~£29,400/yr leaves near-zero after management fees. Needs £800/mo rent to work — Zac advised walking away if landlord won't reduce.

top_objections.text: Setup cost ~£3-5k hard to recover from thin margins
top_objections.zac_response: Acknowledged — setup cost unrecoverable if margins remain minimal

top_objections.text: Sheffield too far from Manchester — geographic barrier to operations
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful manages remotely but Rasheed prefers to operate closer to Manchester

top_questions.text: Does this R2R deal work financially at £1,000/mo rent?
top_questions.answer: No — at £1,000/mo rent + £400/mo bills, overhead ~£16,800/yr. Gross ~£29,400/yr leaves near-zero after 15% management fee. Deal needs ~£800/mo rent to be viable.

top_questions.text: What profit could I make if I negotiate the rent to £800/mo?
top_questions.answer: At £800/mo rent + bills: overhead ~£14,400/yr. Gross ~£29,400/yr = ~£15,000 gross margin before fees — approximately £3-4k/yr net profit at 15% fee.

top_questions.text: Is Sheffield a good area for STL performance?
top_questions.answer: Sheffield has STL potential but is not Stayful's primary market — and adds geographic friction for a Manchester-based operator

what_fell_flat: Landlord's rent demand of £1,000/mo destroys R2R margins
Sheffield distance from Manchester adds operational friction
Setup cost ~£3-5k with no clear payback timeline at thin margins
Zac's honest advice to walk away — no conversion path available

conversion_gaps.gap: R2R viability gate is rent level — 20% discount from landlord is required and very rarely achieved
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Build R2R pre-qualification checklist: check rent vs STL gross revenue ceiling before booking meeting. If landlord asking >75% of STL gross, flag as marginal and advise on rent negotiation strategy before investing full meeting time.

conversion_gaps.gap: Setup costs (~£3-5k) unrecoverable on thin R2R margins
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: For R2R leads, present setup cost as a key viability factor upfront — explore minimalist/phased setup to reduce entry cost and shorten payback period

conversion_gaps.gap: Geographic preference mismatch — lead wants Manchester area, deal is Sheffield
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Capture geographic preference before meeting; if deal location doesn't match, explore whether Stayful can help identify better-located opportunities in lead's preferred area

zac_close_phrase: Walk away if the landlord won't reduce — not much opportunity in this deal
zac_income_intro: Gross ~£29,400/yr at £131/night and 62% occupancy — but rent of £1,000/mo plus bills eats nearly all of it
zac_transition_phrases: not much opportunity in this deal
